revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''日招八幡神社'''（ひまねきはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。 {{神社 |名称 …'
revision.text:
'''日招八幡神社'''（ひまねきはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。 {{神社 |名称 = 日招八幡神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 愛媛県松山市保免中1-1-18 |位置 = |祭神 = 市杵島姫命
湍津姫命
田心姫命
品陀和気命
息長帯姫命 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 崇峻天皇2年(589年) |本殿 = 焼失のため仮宮 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} ==祭神== *[[市杵島姫命]]（いつきしまひめのみこと） *[[湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） *[[田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *品陀和気命（ほんだわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]） *息長帯姫命（おきながたらしひめのみこと、[[神功皇后]]） ==由緒== *崇峻天皇2年（[[589年]]）、[[国司]][[越智益躬]]（小千宿祢益躬）が、[[筑紫国]]の[[宗像大社]]から門島の森に[[比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）を[[勧請]]し、門島比咩神社（かどしまひめじんじゃ）や門乃神社と称した。 *[[大同 (日本)|大同年間]]（806年～809年）に、[[大納言]][[雄友郷]]（かつともきょう）が[[痘瘡]]を患い祈願をしたところ、平癒したため、伊予痘瘡神社として崇敬される。 *仁寿元年（[[851年]]）神位を授受。 *元慶2年（[[876年]]）[[八幡神]]を合祀し、門島座石清水八幡宮と称した。 *元暦元年（[[1184年]]）に[[佐々木三郎盛綱]]（[[佐々木四郎高綱]]ともいわれる）が[[守護]]として入国し、[[砥部町|砥部]][[荏原村 (愛媛県)|荏原]]の森山、大野の一族と戦った時、戦の途中で日没しそうになり日が沈まないよう祈願すると、日が留まり勝利したことで、日招八幡宮と改称した。 *明治5年（[[1872年]]）、[[郷社]]に列する。 *平成13年（[[2001年]]）、火災により本殿、拝殿を焼失した。（現在は神門と仮宮のみ） ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|例大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |} ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄道]]バス[[保免]]下車、徒歩3分。 ==周辺施設== *[[松山中央公園]] *[[石手川]] *[[松山市立余土中学校]] *[[松山市立余土小学校]] ==関連項目== *[[松山八社八幡]]（日招八幡神社は日招八幡として五番社） {{DEFAULTSORT:ひまねきはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:松山市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{改名提案|日招八幡大神社|date=2009年2月}} '''日招八幡神社'''（ひまねきはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。 {{神社 |名称 = 日招八幡神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 愛媛県松山市保免中1-1-18 |位置 = |祭神 = 市杵島姫命
湍津姫命
田心姫命
品陀和気命
息長帯姫命 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 崇峻天皇2年(589年) |本殿 = 焼失のため仮宮 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} ==祭神== *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いつきしまひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *品陀和気命（ほんだわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]） *息長帯姫命（おきながたらしひめのみこと、[[神功皇后]]） ==由緒== *崇峻天皇2年（[[589年]]）、[[国司]][[越智益躬]]（小千宿祢益躬）が、[[筑紫国]]の[[宗像大社]]から門島の森に[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）を[[分霊|勧請]]し、門島比咩神社（かどしまひめじんじゃ）や門乃神社と称した。 *[[大同 (日本)|大同年間]]（806年～809年）に、[[大納言]][[藤原雄友|雄友卿]]が[[天然痘|痘瘡]]を患い祈願をしたところ、平癒したため、伊予痘瘡神社として崇敬される。 *仁寿元年（[[851年]]）神位を授受。 *元慶2年（[[876年]]）[[八幡神]]を合祀し、門島座石清水八幡宮と称した。 *元暦元年（[[1184年]]）に[[佐々木盛綱|佐々木三郎盛綱]]（[[佐々木高綱|佐々木四郎高綱]]ともいわれる）が[[守護]]として入国し、[[砥部町|砥部]][[荏原村 (愛媛県)|荏原]]の森山、大野の一族と戦った時、戦の途中で日没しそうになり日が沈まないよう祈願すると、日が留まり勝利したことで、日招八幡宮と改称した。 *明治5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度|郷社]]に列する。 *平成13年（[[2001年]]）、火災により本殿、拝殿を焼失した。（現在は神門と仮宮のみ） ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|例大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |} ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄道]]バス[[保免]]下車、徒歩3分。 ==周辺施設== *[[松山中央公園]] *[[石手川]] *[[松山市立余土中学校]] *[[松山市立余土小学校]] ==関連項目== *[[松山八社八幡]]（日招八幡神社は日招八幡として五番社） {{DEFAULTSORT:ひまねきはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:松山市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: ページ [[日招八幡神社]] を [[日招八幡大神社]] へ移動: 日招八幡神社は通称だが、正式名称は日招八幡大神社であるため。
revision.text:
{{改名提案|日招八幡大神社|date=2009年2月}} '''日招八幡神社'''（ひまねきはちまんじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。 {{神社 |名称 = 日招八幡神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 愛媛県松山市保免中1-1-18 |位置 = |祭神 = 市杵島姫命
湍津姫命
田心姫命
品陀和気命
息長帯姫命 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 崇峻天皇2年(589年) |本殿 = 焼失のため仮宮 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} ==祭神== *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いつきしまひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *品陀和気命（ほんだわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]） *息長帯姫命（おきながたらしひめのみこと、[[神功皇后]]） ==由緒== *崇峻天皇2年（[[589年]]）、[[国司]][[越智益躬]]（小千宿祢益躬）が、[[筑紫国]]の[[宗像大社]]から門島の森に[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）を[[分霊|勧請]]し、門島比咩神社（かどしまひめじんじゃ）や門乃神社と称した。 *[[大同 (日本)|大同年間]]（806年～809年）に、[[大納言]][[藤原雄友|雄友卿]]が[[天然痘|痘瘡]]を患い祈願をしたところ、平癒したため、伊予痘瘡神社として崇敬される。 *仁寿元年（[[851年]]）神位を授受。 *元慶2年（[[876年]]）[[八幡神]]を合祀し、門島座石清水八幡宮と称した。 *元暦元年（[[1184年]]）に[[佐々木盛綱|佐々木三郎盛綱]]（[[佐々木高綱|佐々木四郎高綱]]ともいわれる）が[[守護]]として入国し、[[砥部町|砥部]][[荏原村 (愛媛県)|荏原]]の森山、大野の一族と戦った時、戦の途中で日没しそうになり日が沈まないよう祈願すると、日が留まり勝利したことで、日招八幡宮と改称した。 *明治5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度|郷社]]に列する。 *平成13年（[[2001年]]）、火災により本殿、拝殿を焼失した。（現在は神門と仮宮のみ） ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|例大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |} ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄道]]バス[[保免]]下車、徒歩3分。 ==周辺施設== *[[松山中央公園]] *[[石手川]] *[[松山市立余土中学校]] *[[松山市立余土小学校]] ==関連項目== *[[松山八社八幡]]（日招八幡神社は日招八幡として五番社） {{DEFAULTSORT:ひまねきはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:松山市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{改名提案|日招八幡大神社|date=2009年2月}} '''日招八幡大神社'''（ひまねきはちまんだいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。 {{神社 |名称 = 日招八幡大神社 |画像 = |所在地 = 愛媛県松山市保免中1-1-18 |位置 = |祭神 = 市杵島姫命
湍津姫命
田心姫命
品陀和気命
息長帯姫命 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 崇峻天皇2年(589年) |本殿 = 焼失のため仮宮 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} ==祭神== *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いつきしまひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *品陀和気命（ほんだわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]） *息長帯姫命（おきながたらしひめのみこと、[[神功皇后]]） ==由緒== *崇峻天皇2年（[[589年]]）、[[国司]][[越智益躬]]（小千宿祢益躬）が、[[筑紫国]]の[[宗像大社]]から門島の森に[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）を[[分霊|勧請]]し、門島比咩神社（かどしまひめじんじゃ）や門乃神社と称した。 *[[大同 (日本)|大同年間]]（806年～809年）に、[[大納言]][[藤原雄友|雄友卿]]が[[天然痘|痘瘡]]を患い祈願をしたところ、平癒したため、伊予痘瘡神社として崇敬される。 *仁寿元年（[[851年]]）神位を授受。 *元慶2年（[[876年]]）[[八幡神]]を合祀し、門島座石清水八幡宮と称した。 *元暦元年（[[1184年]]）に[[佐々木盛綱|佐々木三郎盛綱]]（[[佐々木高綱|佐々木四郎高綱]]ともいわれる）が[[守護]]として入国し、[[砥部町|砥部]][[荏原村 (愛媛県)|荏原]]の森山、大野の一族と戦った時、戦の途中で日没しそうになり日が沈まないよう祈願すると、日が留まり勝利したことで、日招八幡宮と改称した。 *明治5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度|郷社]]に列する。 *平成13年（[[2001年]]）、火災により本殿、拝殿を焼失した。（現在は神門と仮宮のみ） ==境内社== 本殿跡西から北より *佐々木社 *春日社 *火鎮社 *祇園社 *山王大権現 本殿跡東から北より2社が併設（社額の文字が劣化して判別不能） おたた石の東側 *祖霊社 ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|例大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |} ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄道]]バス[[保免]]下車、徒歩3分。 ==その他== *おとよ石 ==周辺施設== *[[松山中央公園]] *[[石手川]] *[[松山市立余土中学校]] *[[松山市立余土小学校]] ==関連項目== *[[松山八社八幡]]（日招八幡神社は日招八幡として五番社） {{DEFAULTSORT:ひまねきはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:愛媛県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:松山市]] {{Shinto-stub}}

'''日招八幡大神社'''（ひまねきはちまんだいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。 {{神社 |名称 = 日招八幡大神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Himanekihachimandaijinjakaridono.JPG|250px|日招八幡大神社仮殿]]

息長帯姫命 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 崇峻天皇2年(589年) |本殿 = 焼失のため仮宮 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} ==祭神== *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いつきしまひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *品陀和気命（ほんだわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]） *息長帯姫命（おきながたらしひめのみこと、[[神功皇后]]） ==由緒== *崇峻天皇2年（[[589年]]）、[[国司]][[越智益躬]]（小千宿祢益躬）が、[[筑紫国]]の[[宗像大社]]から門島の森に[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）を[[分霊|勧請]]し、門島比咩神社（かどしまひめじんじゃ）や門乃神社と称した。 *[[大同 (日本)|大同年間]]（806年～809年）に、[[大納言]][[藤原雄友|雄友卿]]が[[天然痘|痘瘡]]を患い祈願をしたところ、平癒したため、伊予痘瘡神社として崇敬される。 *仁寿元年（[[851年]]）神位を授受。 *元慶2年（[[876年]]）[[八幡神]]を合祀し、門島座石清水八幡宮と称した。 *元暦元年（[[1184年]]）に[[佐々木盛綱|佐々木三郎盛綱]]（[[佐々木高綱|佐々木四郎高綱]]ともいわれる）が[[守護]]として入国し、[[砥部町|砥部]][[荏原村 (愛媛県)|荏原]]の森山、大野の一族と戦った時、戦の途中で日没しそうになり日が沈まないよう祈願すると、日が留まり勝利したことで、日招八幡宮と改称した。 *明治5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度|郷社]]に列する。 *平成13年（[[2001年]]）、火災により本殿、拝殿を焼失した。（現在は神門と仮宮のみ） ==境内社== 本殿跡西から北より *佐々木社 *春日社 *火鎮社 *祇園社 *山王大権現 本殿跡東から北より2社が併設（社額の文字が劣化して判別不能） おたた石の東側 *祖霊社 ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|例大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |} ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄道]]バス[[保免]]下車、徒歩3分。 ==その他== *おとよ石 ==周辺施設== *[[松山中央公園]] *[[石手川]] *[[松山市立余土中学校]] *[[松山市立余土小学校]] ==関連項目== *[[松山八社八幡]]（日招八幡神社は日招八幡として五番社） {{DEFAULTSORT:ひまねきはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:郷社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

息長帯姫命 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 崇峻天皇2年(589年) |本殿 = 焼失のため仮宮 |別名 = |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} '''日招八幡大神社'''（ひまねきはちまんだいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[氏子調#行政単位と郷社|郷社]]。 ==祭神== *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いつきしまひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *品陀和気命（ほんだわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]） *息長帯姫命（おきながたらしひめのみこと、[[神功皇后]]） ==由緒== *崇峻天皇2年（[[589年]]）、[[国司]][[越智益躬]]（小千宿祢益躬）が、[[筑紫国]]の[[宗像大社]]から門島の森に[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）を[[分霊|勧請]]し、門島比咩神社（かどしまひめじんじゃ）や門乃神社と称した。 *[[大同 (日本)|大同年間]]（806年～809年）に、[[大納言]][[藤原雄友|雄友卿]]が[[天然痘|痘瘡]]を患い祈願をしたところ、平癒したため、伊予痘瘡神社として崇敬される。 *仁寿元年（[[851年]]）神位を授受。 *元慶2年（[[876年]]）[[八幡神]]を合祀し、門島座石清水八幡宮と称した。 *元暦元年（[[1184年]]）に[[佐々木盛綱|佐々木三郎盛綱]]（[[佐々木高綱|佐々木四郎高綱]]ともいわれる）が[[守護]]として入国し、[[砥部町|砥部]][[荏原村 (愛媛県)|荏原]]の森山、大野の一族と戦った時、戦の途中で日没しそうになり日が沈まないよう祈願すると、日が留まり勝利したことで、日招八幡宮と改称した。 *明治5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度|郷社]]に列する。 *平成12年（[[2000年]]）、火災により本殿、拝殿を焼失した。（現在は神門と仮宮のみ） *平成26年（[[2014年]]）、再建を開始する。 ==境内社== 本殿跡西から北より *佐々木社 *春日社 *火鎮社 *祇園社 *山王大権現 本殿跡東から北より2社が併設（[[扁額]]の文字が劣化して判別不能） おたた石の東側 *祖霊社 ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|例大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |} ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄道]]バス[[保免]]下車、徒歩3分。 ==その他== *おとよ石 ==周辺施設== *[[松山中央公園]] *[[石手川]] *[[松山市立余土中学校]] *[[松山市立余土小学校]] ==関連項目== *[[松山八社八幡]]（日招八幡神社は日招八幡として五番社） {{DEFAULTSORT:ひまねきはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧郷社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

息長帯姫命 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 崇峻天皇2年(589年) |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 日招八幡宮 |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} '''日招八幡大神社'''（ひまねきはちまんだいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いつきしまひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *品陀和気命（ほんだわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]） *息長帯姫命（おきながたらしひめのみこと、[[神功皇后]]） ==由緒== *崇峻天皇2年（[[589年]]）、[[国司]][[越智益躬]]（小千宿祢益躬）が、[[筑紫国]]の[[宗像大社]]から門島の森に[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）を[[分霊|勧請]]し、門島比咩神社（かどしまひめじんじゃ）や門乃神社と称した。 *[[大同 (日本)|大同年間]]（806年～809年）に、[[大納言]][[藤原雄友|雄友卿]]が[[天然痘|痘瘡]]を患い祈願をしたところ、平癒したため、伊予痘瘡神社として崇敬される。 *仁寿元年（[[851年]]）神位を授受。 *元慶2年（[[876年]]）[[八幡神]]を合祀し、門島座石清水八幡宮と称した。 *元暦元年（[[1184年]]）に[[佐々木盛綱|佐々木三郎盛綱]]（[[佐々木高綱|佐々木四郎高綱]]ともいわれる）が[[守護]]として入国し、[[砥部町|砥部]][[荏原村 (愛媛県)|荏原]]の森山、大野の一族と戦った時、戦の途中で日没しそうになり日が沈まないよう祈願すると、日が留まり勝利したことで、日招八幡宮と改称した。 *明治5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度|郷社]]に列する。 *平成12年（[[2000年]]）、火災により本殿、拝殿を焼失した。 *平成26年（[[2014年]]）、再建を開始。 *平成27年（[[2015年]]）4月20日、新殿祭（新社殿の清祓）、翌21日、遷宮祭。 *平成27年5月9日、竣工奉告祭。 ==境内社== 本殿跡西から北より *佐々木社 *春日社 *火鎮社 *祇園社 *山王大権現 本殿跡東から北より2社が併設（[[扁額]]の文字が劣化して判別不能） おたた石の東側 *祖霊社 ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|例大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |} ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄バス]]保免バス停下車徒歩3分。 ==その他== *おとよ石 ==周辺施設== *[[松山中央公園]] *[[石手川]] *[[松山市立余土中学校]] *[[松山市立余土小学校]] ==関連項目== *[[松山八社八幡]]（日招八幡神社は日招八幡として五番社） {{DEFAULTSORT:ひまねきはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧郷社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 日招八幡大神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Himanekihachimandaijinja 20240525 (3).jpg|250px|日招八幡大神社の社殿]]
再建された社殿 |所在地 = 愛媛県松山市保免中1-1-18 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|49|3|N|132|44|43|E|}} |祭神 = 市杵島姫命
湍津姫命
田心姫命
品陀和気命
息長帯姫命 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 崇峻天皇2年(589年) |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 日招八幡宮 |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} '''日招八幡大神社'''（ひまねきはちまんだいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いつきしまひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *品陀和気命（ほんだわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]） *息長帯姫命（おきながたらしひめのみこと、[[神功皇后]]） ==由緒== *崇峻天皇2年（[[589年]]）、[[国司]][[越智益躬]]（小千宿祢益躬）が、[[筑紫国]]の[[宗像大社]]から門島の森に[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）を[[分霊|勧請]]し、門島比咩神社（かどしまひめじんじゃ）や門乃神社と称した。 *[[大同 (日本)|大同年間]]（806年～809年）に、[[大納言]][[藤原雄友|雄友卿]]が[[天然痘|痘瘡]]を患い祈願をしたところ、平癒したため、伊予痘瘡神社として崇敬される。 *仁寿元年（[[851年]]）神位を授受。 *元慶2年（[[876年]]）[[八幡神]]を合祀し、門島座石清水八幡宮と称した。 *元暦元年（[[1184年]]）に[[佐々木盛綱|佐々木三郎盛綱]]（[[佐々木高綱|佐々木四郎高綱]]ともいわれる）が[[守護]]として入国し、[[砥部町|砥部]][[荏原村 (愛媛県)|荏原]]の森山、大野の一族と戦った時、戦の途中で日没しそうになり日が沈まないよう祈願すると、日が留まり勝利したことで、日招八幡宮と改称した。 *明治5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度|郷社]]に列する。 *平成12年（[[2000年]]）、火災により本殿、拝殿を焼失した。 *平成26年（[[2014年]]）、再建を開始。 *平成27年（[[2015年]]）4月20日、新殿祭（新社殿の清祓）、翌21日、遷宮祭。 *平成27年5月9日、竣工奉告祭。 ==境内社== 本殿跡西から北より *佐々木社 *春日社 *火鎮社 *祇園社 *山王大権現 本殿跡東から北より2社が併設（[[扁額]]の文字が劣化して判別不能） おたた石の東側 *祖霊社 ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|例大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |} ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄バス]]保免バス停下車徒歩3分。 ==その他== *おとよ石 ==周辺施設== *[[松山中央公園]] *[[石手川]] *[[松山市立余土中学校]] *[[松山市立余土小学校]] ==関連項目== *[[松山八社八幡]]（日招八幡神社は日招八幡として五番社） {{DEFAULTSORT:ひまねきはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧郷社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine |Name = Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine |Image = [[File:Himanekihachimandaijinja 20240525 (3).jpg|250px|Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine's rebuilt honden]]
Rebuilt honden |Location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |Coordinates = {{Coord|33|49|3|N|132|44|43|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} |Deity = Ichikishimahime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
Hondawake no Mikoto (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |Annual Festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|神社}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|en|近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * Hondawake no Mikoto (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|en|近代社格制度}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] {{Shinto-stub}}

Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:Original pages]][[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |Annual Festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * Hondawake no Mikoto (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * Hondawake no Mikoto (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代type制度}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代type制度}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Nichishō Hachiman Shrine-qq]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Nichishō Hachiman Shrine-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:suQqqqqq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]]

Rebuilt honden |location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|49|3|N|132|44|43|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ichikishimahime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Rebuilt honden |location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|49|3|N|132|44|43|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ichikishimahime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) [[Category:Shrines in or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=draft}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭|12=draft}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=draft}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=draft}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[en:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]]

culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭|12=draft}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=draft}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=draft}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[en:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Himanekihachimandaijinja 20240525 (3).jpg|250px|Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine's rebuilt honden]]
Rebuilt honden |location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|49|3|N|132|44|43|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ichikishimahime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|12=draft|12=draft}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft|12=draft}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司|12=draft|12=draft}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft|12=draft}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国|12=draft|12=draft}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=draft|12=draft}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭|12=draft|12=draft}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=draft|12=draft}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=draft|12=draft}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[en:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]]

品陀和気命
息長帯姫命 |社格 = 郷社 |創建 = 崇峻天皇2年(589年) |本殿 = [[流造]] |別名 = 日招八幡宮 |例祭 = 10月6日 |神事 = }} '''日招八幡大神社'''（ひまねきはちまんだいじんじゃ）は、[[愛媛県]][[松山市]]にある[[神社]]。神紋は四つ目菱。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。 ==祭神== *[[イチキシマヒメ|市杵島姫命]]（いつきしまひめのみこと） *[[タギツヒメ|湍津姫命]]（たぎつひめのみこと） *[[タキリビメ|田心姫命]]（たごりひめのみこと） *品陀和気命（ほんだわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]） *息長帯姫命（おきながたらしひめのみこと、[[神功皇后]]） ==由緒== *崇峻天皇2年（[[589年]]）、[[国司]][[越智益躬]]（小千宿祢益躬）が、[[筑紫国]]の[[宗像大社]]から門島の森に[[比売神|比咩大神]]（宗像三女神）を[[分霊|勧請]]し、門島比咩神社（かどしまひめじんじゃ）や門乃神社と称した。 *[[大同 (日本)|大同年間]]（806年～809年）に、[[大納言]][[藤原雄友|雄友卿]]が[[天然痘|痘瘡]]を患い祈願をしたところ、平癒したため、伊予痘瘡神社として崇敬される。 *仁寿元年（[[851年]]）神位を授受。 *元慶2年（[[876年]]）[[八幡神]]を合祀し、門島座石清水八幡宮と称した。 *元暦元年（[[1184年]]）に[[佐々木盛綱|佐々木三郎盛綱]]（[[佐々木高綱|佐々木四郎高綱]]ともいわれる）が[[守護]]として入国し、[[砥部町|砥部]][[荏原村 (愛媛県)|荏原]]の森山、大野の一族と戦った時、戦の途中で日没しそうになり日が沈まないよう祈願すると、日が留まり勝利したことで、日招八幡宮と改称した。 *明治5年（[[1872年]]）、[[近代社格制度|郷社]]に列する。 *平成12年（[[2000年]]）、火災により本殿、拝殿を焼失した。 *平成26年（[[2014年]]）、再建を開始。 *平成27年（[[2015年]]）4月20日、新殿祭（新社殿の清祓）、翌21日、遷宮祭。 *平成27年5月9日、竣工奉告祭。 ==境内社== 本殿跡西から北より *佐々木社 *春日社 *火鎮社 *祇園社 *山王大権現 本殿跡東から北より2社が併設（[[扁額]]の文字が劣化して判別不能） おたた石の東側 *祖霊社 ==年中行事== {| class="wikitable" ! 日付　!!　祭祀 |- | 10月6日 || [[例祭|例大祭]]   |- | 10月7日 || [[神幸祭]]   |} ==アクセス== *[[伊予鉄バス]]保免バス停下車徒歩3分。 ==その他== *おとよ石 ==周辺施設== *[[松山中央公園]] *[[石手川]] *[[松山市立余土中学校]] *[[松山市立余土小学校]] ==関連項目== *[[松山八社八幡]]（日招八幡神社は日招八幡として五番社） {{DEFAULTSORT:ひまねきはちまんしんしや}} [[Category:松山市の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:愛媛県の旧郷社]] {{Shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[日招八幡大神社]] to [[Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Himanekihachimandaijinja 20240525 (3).jpg|250px|Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine's rebuilt honden]]
Rebuilt honden |location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|49|3|N|132|44|43|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ichikishimahime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gōsha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishimahime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishimahime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[en:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=draft}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi|ja|国司|12=draft}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|ja|宗像大社|12=draft}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|ja|筑紫国|12=draft}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=draft}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=draft}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinkōsai|ja|神幸祭|12=draft}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=draft}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=draft}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=draft}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Ehime]] [[Category:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[en:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]]

Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|qq=}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Himanekihachimandaijinja 20240525 (3).jpg|250px|Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine's rebuilt honden]]
Rebuilt honden |location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|49|3|N|132|44|43|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
[[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 例祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkōsai]] (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|qq=}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[Category:Shrines in Matsuyama]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Himanekihachimandaijinja 20240525 (3).jpg|250px|Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine's rebuilt honden]]
Rebuilt honden |location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33|49|3|N|132|44|43|E|region:JP-38|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イチキシマヒメ|1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 例祭}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkōsai]] (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|qq=}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|qq=}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|qq=}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]]

Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 |prefecture = {{ill|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkōsai]] (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|qq=|1=Matsuyama Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529755}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|qq=|1=Ishite River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9071833}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|qq=|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529814}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|qq=|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116125335}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|qq=|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529766}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Nichishō Hachiman Shrine]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11505341}}

The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge).
