Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 |location = {{ill|Matsuyama|qid=Q200022}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684}} }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|qq=|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkōsai]] (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|qq=|1=Matsuyama Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529755}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|qq=|1=Ishite River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9071833}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|qq=|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529814}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|qq=|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116125335}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|qq=|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529766}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11505341}}

Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 |location = {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkōsai]] (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|1=Matsuyama Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529755}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|1=Ishite River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9071833}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529814}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116125335}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529766}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11505341}}

Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkōsai]] (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|1=Matsuyama Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529755}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|1=Ishite River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9071833}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529814}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116125335}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529766}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11505341}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11505341]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Himanekihachimandaijinjakaridono.JPG |location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.8175|N|132.745278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 |location = {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkōsai]] (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|1=Matsuyama Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529755}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|1=Ishite River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9071833}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529814}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116125335}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529766}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] {{wikidata link|Q11505341}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11505341]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Himanekihachimandaijinjakaridono.JPG |location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.8175|N|132.745278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 |location = {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), [[:en:Kokushi (official)|Kokushi]] Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the [[:en:Munakata Taisha|Munakata Taisha]] deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from [[:en:Tsukushi Province|Tsukushi Province]] to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || [[:en:Shinko-shiki|Shinkōsai]] (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|1=Matsuyama Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529755}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|1=Ishite River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9071833}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529814}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116125335}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529766}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11505341}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinkōsai}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|1=Matsuyama Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529755}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|1=Ishite River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9071833}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529814}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116125335}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529766}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11505341}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Himanekihachimandaijinjakaridono.JPG |location = 1-1-18 Hommenaka, Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture |coordinates = {{Coord|33.8175|N|132.745278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto
Tagitsuhime no Mikoto
Tagorihime no Mikoto
{{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Emperor Ōjin)
Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 |location = {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinkōsai}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|1=Matsuyama Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529755}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|1=Ishite River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9071833}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529814}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|1=Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116125335}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529766}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11505341}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) |Rank = Former Gō-sha |Established = 589 CE (Suiko 2) |Main Building = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alias = Himaneki Hachimangū |festival = October 6 |location = {{ill|Matsuyama|WD=Q200022|1=Matsuyama}} |architecture_style = {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Himaneki Hachiman Daijin Shrine''' (日招八幡大神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Matsuyama, Ehime Prefecture. The shrine's crest is the Yotsume-bishi (four-eyed lozenge). It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following deities: * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|ja|市杵島姫命|1=Ichikishima-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|ja|湍津姫命|1=Tagitsuhime|2=ja|3=タギツヒメ|4=zh|5=多岐都比賣命|6=fr|7=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagorihime no Mikoto|ja|田心姫命|1=Tagorihime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タキリビメ}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (Emperor Ōjin) * Okinagatarashihime no Mikoto (Empress Jingū) == History == * In 589 CE (Suiko 2), {{ill|Kokushi (official)|en|Kokushi (official)|WD=Q1351234|lt=Kokushi|1=Kokushi (official)}} Ochi Masumi brought the divided spirit of the {{ill|Munakata Taisha|en|Munakata Taisha|WD=Q704962|lt=Munakata Taisha|1=Munakata Taisha}} deities (Munakata Sanjoshin) from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|WD=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}} to Kadojima Forest, establishing the shrine as Kadojima Hime Shrine or Kado no Shrine. * During the {{ill|Daidō|ja|大同 (元号)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} era (806–809), court noble Fujiwara no Yutomo prayed for recovery from smallpox, resulting in the shrine gaining fame as Iyo Tōsō Shrine. * In 851 (Ninju 1), the shrine was granted divine Rank. * In 876 (Gangyō 2), the shrine incorporated Hachiman as a deity and became known as Kadojima Iwashimizu Hachimangū. * In 1184 (Genryaku 1), Sasaki Saburō Moritsuna (or Sasaki Shirō Takatsuna) prayed for victory in battle, stopping the sun's descent, leading to the renaming of the shrine to Himaneki Hachimangū. * In 1872 (Meiji 5), it was designated a {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. * In 2000 (Heisei 12), a fire destroyed the honden and haiden. * Reconstruction began in 2014 (Heisei 26), culminating in a new honden and haiden in April 2015, with the official dedication taking place on May 9, 2015. == Subsidiary Shrines == Subsidiary shrines located west to north of the honden: * Sasaki Shrine * Kasuga Shrine * Hiwatashi Shrine * Gion Shrine * Sannō Daigongen Shrines east to north of the former honden site: * Two unnamed shrines with deteriorated plaques East of the Otatashi Stone: * Sorei Shrine == Annual Events == {| class="wikitable" ! Date !! Festival |- | October 6 || {{ill|Reisai|lt=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reitaisai|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} (Grand Festival) |- | October 7 || {{ill|Shinko-shiki|en|Shinko-shiki|WD=Q123907414|lt=Shinkōsai|1=Shinko-shiki}} (Portable Shrine Procession) |} == Access == * 3 minutes' walk from the Homen Bus Stop on the Iyo Railway Bus Line. == Nearby Landmarks == * {{ill|Matsuyama Central Park|ja|松山中央公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Central Park|1=Matsuyama Central Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529755}} * {{ill|Ishite River|ja|石手川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishite River|1=Ishite River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9071833}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|ja|松山市立余土中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Junior High School|1=Q11529814|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529814}} * {{ill|Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|ja|松山市立余土小学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Municipal Yodo Elementary School|1=Q116125335|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q116125335}} == See Also == * {{ill|Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|ja|松山八社八幡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|1=Matsuyama Eight Shrines of Hachiman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529766}} (Himaneki Hachiman Shrine is the fifth) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|日招八幡大神社|version=100486465|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:日招八幡大神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11505341}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]
